subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the concept of fractals and self-similar patterns
Construct Sierpinski Carpet and Sierpinski Triangle
Understand Koch Snowflake and pattern growth
Identify faces, edges and vertices of solids
Understand nets of solids and construct them
Understand projections and shadows of solids
Draw simple solids using isometric grids

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Fractals
topics_covered: Examples such as fern leaves and trees

lesson_plan.title: Sierpinski Carpet
teaching_strategy: Board demonstration of Step 0, Step 1 and Step 2
student_activities: Draw Step 1 and Step 2 of Sierpinski Carpet

lesson_plan.title: Sierpinski Triangle
student_activities: Draw Sierpinski triangle up to Step 2

lesson_plan.title: Koch Snowflake
topics_covered: Growth of sides in each step

lesson_plan.title: Visualising Solids
student_activities: Observe objects from top, front and side

lesson_plan.title: Faces, Edges and Vertices
student_activities: Count faces, edges and vertices of a cube

lesson_plan.title: Nets of Solids
topics_covered: Meaning of net of a solid
student_activities: Cut and fold to form cube

lesson_plan.title: Projections and Isometric Drawing
topics_covered: Top view, front view and side view
student_activities: Identify top, front and side views of objects

teacher_tip: Use physical models and hands-on drawing activities to help students better visualise fractals, solids, and projections.
